title: Quiz: Period Finding and Factoring
description: Test your understanding of the period finding reduction

questions.question: The period r of f(x) = a^x mod N is the smallest positive integer such that:
questions.explanation: The period r satisfies a^r ≡ 1 (mod N), meaning a^r mod N = 1.

questions.question: For the period r to help factor N, r must be:
questions.explanation: We need r even so we can compute a^(r/2), and a^(r/2) ≢ ±1 to get non-trivial factors.

questions.question: Given period r, factors are found using:
questions.explanation: We compute gcd(a^(r/2) - 1, N) and gcd(a^(r/2) + 1, N) to find factors.

questions.question: If gcd(a, N) > 1 when choosing random a, what happens?
questions.explanation: If gcd(a, N) > 1, that gcd is already a non-trivial factor of N - we're done!

questions.question: Classical period finding requires exponential time, but quantum period finding is polynomial.
questions.explanation: This is the key quantum speedup! Classical: O(√N) = O(2^(n/2)). Quantum: O(n³).
